reviews.review_title: VERY GOOD GAME BUT WHERE ARE THE DRAGONS
reviews.review_description:
This game is wonderful a challenging escape room the levels get more complicated each time you advance I got stuck in many parts but it was incredibly satisfying when I solved it and everything worked I was fascinated by most of the mechanics some work better than others but the variety is hilarious. 

The dragons thing....
I was trying to complete the achievements of your game but did not find the dragon collectibles I could only find the one in the great hall I do not know if it is bugged or if they are not set because I can not find it or if they are could give me a clue to find them. 

Thanks

The game is very buggy, with lots of herky-jerky movements and completely random physics (sometimes you let go of an item and it just falls, sometimes it flies across the room).  Add to that the idea that you have to *pay* for a hint, and it's an absolute pass for me.

Also, I don't know if it's another bug or part of the actual design of the room, but when "The Great Room" first started, somehow I already had a pink-flaming spear, but by the time I found the first three cystals, somehow that spear either disappeared or had gone out.  I don't know if I was supposed to find the pink crystal (and if so, how - I felt like I solved everything available) to regain the pink flame, or if that spear was supposed to remain available to me, to complete the ten-step light sequence.  Either way, made for a very frustrating experience.
You can't move around so if your in a small space you can't play this game . When you try to throw spears they fly different ways. Not worth my time

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback.  Normally, players use the controller to teleport around the room so a large play space is definitely not required.  Many players play this game while sitting down in fact.  Also, the game doesn’t require you to throw spears; did you discover that you can shoot fireballs from a lit up spear?

reviews.review_description:
I really enjoyed playing and was impressed with the mechanics and puzzles.  Unfortunately for me, I couldn’t handle the motion needed for the second part of the fifth room.  I’m sorry I won’t be able to finish it.

Four stars anyway because I think it was great fun and challenging until then.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your review and your feedback.  We have been talking about major changes that we want to make to the fifth room to make it flow better with the previous four.  We intend for this to be a casual game and the "motion" parts seem to move beyond that.  When we implement these changes, we would love your feedback on them.  Thank you so much for playing "The Great Escape: Dragon's Dungeon"!

reviews.review_description:
This is a fans take on an escape room. 

If you like escape rooms this one is fun! 

Yes as some have claimed you do get stuck. Just get at it, if your to that point of giving up there is a hint button, if that’s not enough…Google helps.

I absolutely love this game.  I was stumped many times, but in a good way.  However, I can't finish the game.  In the room where you need to fly the dragon, I can't figure out how to make the dragon fly.  It changes my perspective, so that I am looking at the dragon from above, and it makes me extremely motion sick.  Other than that, fun game!

developer_response.body: Hello!  We are really glad you enjoyed the game!  We are making improvements based on feedback such as yours.  Frankly, we believe we need to re-work the Dungeon room for a variety of reasons.  When the update is made, we hope you will come back to finish the game!  Thank you so much for playing!

reviews.review_description: Love this game

reviews.review_title: Starts strong, then starts to meander
reviews.review_description:
Thanks for telling me how to move. I found it a bit non intuitive, but got used to it after a fashion. I’m left handed, so grabbing everything with my right was not natural. I also found that sometimes the left grip button actually picked things up.

The game looks fantastic. Very well done.

First room (jail) is a good intro.

I’d rate the second room 4*, detracting for some physics issues, but making allowances for it being a game preview. Difficulty was about perfect. Very good puzzles. Very well done.

I’d rate the next room 1*. The puzzles were insultingly simple, especially the  maze and othello that allows pieces be placed anywhere. I was a little surprised there was no tic tac toe :) . Then the shooting galley stage was actually pretty bad, especially the hammer toss that makes me constantly turn around for another hammer. I couldn’t get past the flaming arrows without exploiting (pushing the shield way out into the middle of the room).

The next room (kings chamber) was much better, than the war room. It had a pretty clever mechanism in it as well. Ultimately though, it was way too easy. And why just dump all the clues into a big box? Why not scatter them around and have other puzzles to obtain them? I’d say 3*.

The final room I tried was the dragon room. I agree with other comments that the dragon flight was slightly nauseating, and the minion battle is too broken to play. A third puzzle was a game like lemmings, trying to get a warrior to cross the room. That was well done I thought, but not an escape room. Overall I’d have to say 1* in the shape its in. Getting the warrior across the room has good potential to be its own game.

I’ll restate what I said in the first review draft; you are an escape room, a shooting gallery, a saber slicer, lemmings, flight simulator, and whatever minions was supposed to be. Interesting idea to mix them all into one game, but I don’t think it works. There may be audiences for each of those game types individually, but you are limiting yourself to players who like all of them. I personally think you should split them off into separate games.

developer_response.body:
Update: Thank you so much for your updated feedback.  The feedback is thoughtful and will be helpful for us to improve the game.  With respect to "Dungeon", we agree with you and many of our other players that this room lacks a lot of polish.  We are in the process of completely overhauling this room into a new version that is more consistent with "The Great Room" and "King's Quarters".  We also enjoyed putting together the "Lemmings-style" game, but it doesn't quite fit the theme we are looking for.  We also appreciate all of your other comments as well.  We hope that you will come back and try the revamped "Dungeon" room when it is completed.  Also, we just released a small update for Christmas.

Thank you again for playing and giving your feedback!

=======================

Thank you for your feedback!  We will work on increasing the highlighting of the buttons in the UI.  Also, we intend to implement continuous movement as a movement option within the game.  Currently, movement is done by using the button under your left middle finger to teleport around.  Please let us know if this works for you!  Good luck!

reviews.review_description: I really like this game. Difficult to solve, but had some good advice from the dev, Brian, who has said that on main release, there will be some form of hint system. This would be ideal for those of us who need that. Some clever ideas encorporated into this game. A save option more often would be good. Lessens the frustration!
This game is awesome! It is challenging which makes it even more fun. Love it!

Looks a good game but I'm stuck on only the second room. I've had two goes at it and I got a bit further the second time before getting stuck again. I've had an idea for something to try on my 3rd attempt but if that doesn't work not sure what I'll do. I know the puzzles wouldn't be fun if they were too easy but maybe some sort of hint system would be helpful for novices like myself.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your feedback!!  We would love to hear about where you got stuck each of those times to see if there's a way we can improve the in-game hints.  Can you tell us where you got stuck?

reviews.review_description:
Had to go ahead and write played the first 2 levels so far and love it. It looks amazing and can’t wait to play more. Will update review with more details when finished with game.

Update finished and love it can’t wait to see more. Hope it becomes a never ending escape game.

This game is a lot of fun. The puzzles are mostly challenging but not too challenging. I am having trouble with the War Room. The Hammer Throw and Sword were pretty easy. It took me FOREVER to shoot down enough barrels with the spear. I cannot figure out the Shield though. I keep blocking fireballs with the shield but not enough I guess? I'm not even sure if I'm doing it right. The hint video for that part of the War Room only shows the barrels.

Overall though, I highly recommend this game and I cannot wait to see what it will be like when it graduates beyond the App Lab.

By the way, the ball maze and Othello mini games are pretty simple, but I enjoy them each time. The other two puzzles in that room (slide puzzle and pentominos) are only slightly more difficult for me, but it's a little infuriating that I have to redo them again each time I load the game before I can try again to block enough fireballs or shoot enough barrels to get past the War Room.

I really love this type of game. Keep up the great work!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your feedback and thoughtful comments!  We will use this feedback to make improvements and expand upon the game.  We hope you will continue to play more as these updates are rolled out.  Thank you for playing!

reviews.review_description: I stuck in the warroom. Cant finish the speer and shield. No way. I payed money for a hint but all I got is what I allready know. Shoot the barrels. Thats not okay. Sorry. I quit after trying hard.

This game could be a lot of fun if it just wasn't so buggy! The actual rooms themselves are really fun! Sure, some of the puzzles need to be easier (I'm looking at you barrel shooting game) or harder, but overall it they were really fun!

Now what wasn't so fun was the camera and movement. I don't tend to get motion sick in VR but I can't play for very long in this game. Also some of the physics and collision needs to be worked on. Luckily there's not much you need to throw anyway but when you do throw something, it will either go really far or nowhere. Rarely will it go where you wanted it to.
Then if you move around too fast or if there's a lot loaded in, the game gets REALLY choppy. 

Let's break it up by level now.
Level 1: alright but just too short.
Level 2: Pretty fun and had some fairly challenging puzzles
Level 3: overall fun but the shooting mini games could use some work.
Level 4: fun but simple. The scroll puzzle could be a little more difficult.
Level 5: Don't even bother. It's confusing as it doesn't really point you to what's you need to do. The first challenge is easy once you figure out what's happening and doing the challenge is really fun. The second challenge needs to have some kind of instructions for the controls at very least. Even once I figured those out, it still made me motion sick. The third challenge is just awful to the point that I couldn't make it further. It's not really clear as to what's happening, or what your goal is, or even what your actually doing for that matter. And before you can even figure it out, there are so many enemies spawned in that the game becomes just still shots every few seconds. I thought flipping the switch to turn it off and back on would reset it but no. After slowly crawling my way back to the lever, I turned it off and the smoothness came back. I then flipped the lever again to try the challenge again but instead of starting from the beginning of the challenge, it picked up right where it left off. All the previous enemies spawned back and the frame rate went back to awful in a split second.

Last thing, it needs a save feature.

I truly wish I could give this game a higher rating because the level design and most of the puzzles are actually really fun, but until this game fixes all of it's big issues (especially the Dungeon level), I just can't fairly give it a better rating.

developer_response.body: I really appreciate your feedback and we think your feedback is very thoughtful and helpful.  We will work to incorporate changes based on your feedback and we hope you will play it again when the changes have been implemented!  Thank you very much!

reviews.review_description: For the fact it is just in testing phase it is pretty good so far enjoying it a lot.
I need help geting the blue gem i can get all the other ones

Was enjoying the game but the the war room just finished it for me. The barrels & shield! Really??? 2 days spent on these was more than enough. I've been playing frustrating games for years haha and many controllers have been thrown across the floor but either I'm getting way too old or there is a glitch. I've tried everything with these two. Everything. With the spear, where does it keep disappearing to?? haha Enough is enough. Shame. A save point might have helped a little ;-)

reviews.review_title: Started off great, but broken last level
reviews.review_description:
I really enjoyed my time with this game. Right up until the final Dungeon room that is. The final room was such a horribly tedious and frustrating experience. The first part was clear enough (although time consuming to position so many little ladders and platforms).  
The second part looked like it would be simple enough, but there was no instructions on how to make the dragon fly. All I could do was make it run around on the floor and breath fire. After about 5 minutes of that it suddenly started flying, and I still have no idea how or why. Flying the dragon was also VERY motion sickness inducing.
And then the third part is so utterly confusing that I don't even know what to do. Hundreds of tiny skeletons spawn and freeze the game after a few minutes. Sometimes I seem to be able to control a skeleton and hit other skeletons or the spawn points (but sometimes I can't). But I can't get very far before the game becomes unresponsive. And why am I controlling a skeleton and not the dwarf hero (who just stands there and does nothing)? I read all the scrolls by the levers, but I don't really understand what's happening. 
I've never gotten past this point, so I don't know what the 4th lever does. And add this all to the fact that getting to the third lever takes a long time, and you have to start all over every time it freezes. And there is supposed to be a hint system, but every hint just seems to play a trailer for the game without any actual information. I really loved the other levels, but the end just ruins the whole experience.

Agree with redeye70.   Got to the war room and can't do the spear and shield. Gave up after many tries as you have to start the level from the begining a shame really as it could have been a great game.
